·

40 Eridani

A · 5B · 0C · skip-list
2021AJ....162...53M 2021 B, C Speckle Interferometry at the U.S. Naval Observatory. XXIV Revised B–C visual orbit (P 233.2 yr, e 0.414) + dynamical masses (B 0.558, C 0.198 M☉) that supersede the adopted Mason/Bond 2017 elements; falls outside current ±σ. Feeds db/binary_orbits.json + Principia n-body. No arXiv (IOP paywall).
1997MNRAS.284..803S 1997 A Rotation, turbulence and evidence for magnetic fields in southern dwarfs Source of the ~37 d rotation period that conflicts with the adopted ~42 d (Burrows 2024); full text needed to weigh the 37–42 d spread for rotation_period_days. Pre-arXiv (OUP).
1974AJ.....79..819H 1974 C Astrometric study of four visual binaries Holds the B–C mass ratio that Mason 2017/2021 use to split the dynamical mass-sum into 40 Eri C's mass (0.198–0.204 M☉); verifying it propagates straight to C's cfg mass. Pre-arXiv.
2011A&A...531A...8J 2011 A Chromospheric activities and kinematics for solar type dwarfs and subgiants The recommended activity value log R'HK = −4.99 lives in the CDS/VizieR Table 4 only — the arXiv preprint (1103.0584) omits the per-star table. Manual VizieR lookup needed to verify the adopted number.
2409.10679 2025 A (+ b) SPORES-HWO. II. Companion Mass Limits and Updated Planet Properties for 120 Future Exoplanet Imaging Targets Reportedly (§D.6) gives 40 Eri A rotation P = 42.322 ± 0.015 d — a formal σ vs our σ-less ~42 d (rotation_period_days) — plus an independent 666-RV confirmation of the b refutation. Has arXiv (2409.10679) but ar5iv fails to render it (stub) and the value is appendix-only, so the number could NOT be auto-verified — fetch §D.6 to confirm.

(none)

The white-dwarf-B parameters (Bond 2017) and the triple-system outer orbit (Mason 2017) remain the adopted anchors; no unfetchable paper supersedes them.

Alpha Centauri Proxima

A · 0B · 0C · skip-list

(none)

(none)

- Maccone 2014 (radio bridge engineering) - Rai 2022 (intensity interferometry simulation) - Houelle 2026 (conference proceedings) - Hill 2023 (catalog) - Armas-Vázquez 2023 (prebiotic chemistry, out of scope) - Sadovski 2018 (cosmic rays near Proxima b) - Duvvuri 2025 (EUV stellar library)

LUHMAN-16

A · 1B · 0C · skip-list
2020ApJ...894...42M 2020 A, B Detection of Polarization due to Cloud Bands in the Nearby Luhman 16 Brown Dwarf Binary First direct detection of longitudinal cloud BANDS on Luhman 16 A (H-band VLT/NaCo polarimetry, p_A 0.031%). Could overturn the current "near-uniform / minimal weather" cloud_morphology decision for A. No usable arXiv (IOP only).

(none)

The A–B masses/orbit, Teff, variability amplitudes, and the young Oceanus-MG age are all anchored on arXiv-readable papers (Bedin 2024, Biller 2024/2025, de Regt 2026); no other unfetchable paper changes a value.

Tau Cet

A · 2B · 0C · skip-list
2004MNRAS.351L..54G 2004 disk A debris disc around τ Ceti: a massive analogue to the Kuiper Belt Sole source for the disk mass (1.2 M⊕), dust temperature (60 K) and outer radius (55 AU) rows; MacGregor 2016 does not measure dust mass or fix the outer edge. Full text holds the SCUBA flux→mass derivation. No arXiv (MNRAS Letter).
2004A&A...426..601D 2004 star VLTI near-IR interferometric observations of Vega-like stars — Radius and age of α PsA, β Leo, β Pic, ε Eri and τ Cet The recommended ~10 Gyr isochrone age anchor, currently held only via the Korolik 2023 cache; full text has the age fit underpinning the documented divergence (10 vs 5.8 Gyr). No arXiv.

(none)

The disk geometry (inner/outer edge, inclination) is anchored on MacGregor 2016 (arXiv-readable); the planet roster is anchored on Feng 2017 + Figueira 2025 (both arXiv-readable). No other unfetchable paper changes a value.

TRAPPIST-1

A · 9B · 12C · skip-list
2026NatAs.tmp...88. 2026 b, c The innermost planets in the TRAPPIST-1 system do not have thick atmospheres Likely a Nature Astronomy publication consolidating Greene 2023 / Zieba 2023 / Ducrot 2025; may tighten atmosphere upper limits
2026NatAs.tmp...65G 2026 b, c No thick atmosphere around TRAPPIST-1 b and c from JWST thermal phase curves Likely the published version of 2509.02128 (Ducrot 2025); confirm if there are revised constraints
2024ESS.....510106L 2024 f TRAPPIST-1 Atmospheric Reconnaissance with JWST: First Look at the Habitable-Zone Exoplanet TRAPPIST-1 f with NIRISS Only direct JWST observation of f, currently cited from ADS abstract only. Full text could clarify atmosphere constraints.
2024ESS.....562710L 2024 c Potential Atmospheric Compositions of TRAPPIST-1 c constrained by JWST Possibly a precursor / conference version of Lincowski 2023 (arXiv 2308.05899) — confirm consistency
2025epsc.conf..605N 2025 b, c, e (?) Predicted atmospheric evolutionary pathways for the TRAPPIST-1 planets Could supply system-wide evolutionary framework
2025EGUGA..2718054S 2025 b Modelling surface mineral diversity of atmosphere-free rocky exoplanets Directly informs surface_tint_rgb_hex_primary for b — mineralogy modeling for airless rocky exoplanets
2024EPSC...17..649T 2024 b, c Reflectance and Emission Modelling of Airless Exoplanets Informs surface colors / albedo for airless b and c
2024MNRAS.530L..13P 2024 g, h Fundamentals for habitable scenarios for Earth-like planets in the TRAPPIST-1 system Could refine the snowball/ocean choices for outer planets
2018AJ....155..239J 2018 h Dynamical Constraints on Nontransiting Planets Orbiting TRAPPIST-1 Constrains additional resonance partners that could affect h's tidal evolution

2024AGUFMP11E.3011L 2024 e Coupled Atmospheric Chemistry, Radiation, and Dynamics of an Exoplanet (TRAPPIST-1 e) Refinement of GCM input for e's atmosphere
2024EPSC...17..168C 2024 e Haze Optical Depth in Exoplanet Atmospheres Varies with Rotation Rate Cloud / haze visibility modeling
2024ESS.....562504V 2024 e Who Run the World? (Flares): Ozone production in steam atmospheres during M-dwarf flares O₃ visual tint refinement
2024ESS.....562907W 2024 e What If The Earth Orbited TRAPPIST-1 Instead of the Sun? Pedagogical / context
2024eas..conf..697C / 2024EGUGA..2611513C 2024 e, g Impact of CO2 on water outgassing on rocky planets around TRAPPIST-1 Atmosphere composition refinement for e/g
2024ApJ...970L...4D 2024 h Updated Spectral Characteristics for the Ultracool Dwarf TRAPPIST-1 Could update Teff / spectral type used in visual styling
2022AGUFM.P35C1897A 2022 h Transition from XUV Driven Ion Escape to Hydrodynamic Escape Atmospheric retention regime for h
2020EPSC...14..355A 2020 c, g Assessing the habitability of observed Super Earths Context
2018AGUFM.P44B..06M 2018 d, f Insights into the atmospheres of the TRAPPIST-1 planets from the laboratory Aerosol / chemistry lab work
2025epsc.conf..580B 2025 b High-Resolution Transmission Spectroscopy of Venus: A Proxy for Atmospheric Characterization Methodology
2025epsc.conf..178P 2025 d TRAPPIST-1 d: A Case Study in Atmospheric Loss Likely conference abstract of Piaulet-Ghorayeb 2025 (already covered via 2508.08416)
2025EGUGA..27.6151S 2025 e Is It Possible to Detect Airless Exomoons Through Thermal Phase Curves? Methodology

These are mostly conference proceedings (BAAS, AAS, AGU, EAS, EGU, EPSC) that summarize already-published work, plus VizieR data catalogs. 43 such papers exist across the 6 planets' bibliographies. Listed exhaustively in phase3/trappist_1/_manual-followup-source.json. Generally safe to skip; they cite the same primary references already read.

40 Eridani A
2011A&A...531A...8J2011Jenkins et al. 2011 — log R'HK activity (VizieR catalog, no ar5iv)

55 Cnc
2024Natur.630..609H2024Hu et al. 2024 — JWST MIRI/NIRCam secondary-eclipse evidence for a volatile (CO/CO2) secondary atmosphere on 55 Cnc e (Nature; ar5iv likely empty -> manual_followup)

61 Vir
1996ApJ...457L..99B1996Baliunas et al. 1996 — Ca II HK rotation 29 d (original rotation source; no arXiv)

AU Mic
2000MNRAS.319.1147T2000Tsikoudi & Kellett 2000 — ROSAT X-ray Lx 2.24e29 erg/s (RECOMMENDED activity; no arXiv, ADS/text value-check)
2019AAS...23325941W2019White et al. 2019 — CHARA interferometry R 0.75 (radius alt; UNREFEREED AAS abstract, no arXiv)

Delta Pavonis
1996AJ....111..439H1996Henry et al. 1996 — chromospheric activity log R'HK (no arXiv; VizieR/ADS value-check)
1998A&AS..132..155H1998Hunsch et al. 1998 — ROSAT nearby-stars X-ray catalog (no arXiv; VizieR)
2013A&A...555A..11E2013Eiroa et al. 2013 — DUNES debris disk (disks_curated; arXiv resolve when needed)

eps Eri
2006ApJ...648..607C2006Croll et al. 2006 — MOST photometric rotation 11.35/11.55 d (RECOMMENDED rotation; no arXiv, ADS value-check)
2005ApJS..159..141V2005Valenti & Fischer 2005 — SPOCS spectroscopy, mass/age/Teff alternatives (no arXiv)

eps Ind A
2009A&A...505..205D2009Demory et al. 2009 — VLTI/VINCI interferometric radius/Teff/L (VizieR J/A+A/505/205)

Fomalhaut
2005AN....326...25D2005Davis et al. 2005 — SUSI interferometry R 1.744 / Teff 8819 (uncorrected; alt, no arXiv)
1997MNRAS.286..604D1997Dunkin et al. 1997 — high-res spectroscopy [Fe/H] -0.03 (alt; no arXiv)
2005Natur.435.1067K2005Kalas et al. 2005 — HST/ACS first resolved eccentric main ring at 133 AU, e 0.11, sharp inner edge (Nature, no arXiv)

HD 69830
2006Natur.441..305L2006Lovis et al. 2006 — 3-Neptune discovery, age 4-10 Gyr (planet host context; old astro-ph, no ar5iv)
2007ApJ...658..584L2007Lisse et al. 2007 — dust mineralogy (disrupted P/D-type asteroid debris); ar5iv renders no full text

proxima-cen-b
2025AJ....169..171H failed2025Bioverse: Giant Magellan Telescope and Extremely Large Telescope Direct Imaging and High-resolution Spectroscopy Assessment—Surveying Exo-Earth O<SUB>2</SUB> and Testing the Habitable Zone Oxygen Hypothesis

tau Cet
2004A&A...426..601D2004Di Folco et al. 2004 — VLTI/VINCI + isochrone age ~10 Gyr (RECOMMENDED age; no arXiv, value via Korolik cache)
1996ApJ...457L..99B1996Baliunas et al. 1996 — Ca II HK chromospheric rotation ~34 d (rotation alternative; no arXiv)
2004MNRAS.351L..54G2004Greaves et al. 2004 — SCUBA 850um first detection of the tau Cet disk (no arXiv)

trappist-1-b
2023ApJ...959...64H failed2023Characterizing the Near-infrared Spectra of Flares from TRAPPIST-1 during JWST Transit Spectroscopy Observations
2022A&A...665A..19G failed2022HST/WFC3 transmission spectroscopy of the cold rocky planet TRAPPIST-1h
2022ApJ...941L..35W failed2022Can 1D Radiative-equilibrium Models of Faculae Be Used for Calculating Contamination of Transmission Spectra?

trappist-1-c
2023ApJ...959...64H failed2023Characterizing the Near-infrared Spectra of Flares from TRAPPIST-1 during JWST Transit Spectroscopy Observations
2022A&A...665A..19G failed2022HST/WFC3 transmission spectroscopy of the cold rocky planet TRAPPIST-1h

trappist-1-d
2023ApJ...959...64H failed2023Characterizing the Near-infrared Spectra of Flares from TRAPPIST-1 during JWST Transit Spectroscopy Observations
2022A&A...665A..19G failed2022HST/WFC3 transmission spectroscopy of the cold rocky planet TRAPPIST-1h

trappist-1-e
2023ApJ...959...64H failed2023Characterizing the Near-infrared Spectra of Flares from TRAPPIST-1 during JWST Transit Spectroscopy Observations
2022A&A...665A..19G failed2022HST/WFC3 transmission spectroscopy of the cold rocky planet TRAPPIST-1h

trappist-1-g
2023ApJ...959...64H failed2023Characterizing the Near-infrared Spectra of Flares from TRAPPIST-1 during JWST Transit Spectroscopy Observations
2022A&A...665A..19G failed2022HST/WFC3 transmission spectroscopy of the cold rocky planet TRAPPIST-1h
2022ApJ...941L..35W failed2022Can 1D Radiative-equilibrium Models of Faculae Be Used for Calculating Contamination of Transmission Spectra?

trappist-1-h
2022A&A...665A..19G failed2022HST/WFC3 transmission spectroscopy of the cold rocky planet TRAPPIST-1h
2023ApJ...959...64H failed2023Characterizing the Near-infrared Spectra of Flares from TRAPPIST-1 during JWST Transit Spectroscopy Observations

Vega
2010ApJ...708...71Y2010Yoon et al. 2010 — NPOI gravity-darkened + rotating evolutionary; mass 2.135, age 455, [M/H] -0.4 (alt; no arXiv, IOP only)
2006ApJ...636..426P2006Pease, Drake & Kashyap 2006 — Chandra X-ray non-detection, L_X < 2e25 erg/s (no arXiv)

  1. ADS bibcodes like 2026NatAs.tmp...88. → search at
  2. Nature Astronomy 2026 papers: probably have arXiv preprints
  3. Conference papers (EPSC, EGU, AGU): usually have abstracts only;
  4. For Claude for Chrome: navigate to the ADS abstract page, then
  5. After paste, the workflow is: add the text to a new file under
